input.content:
# OpenOCD probe: which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable
# on the running vsim DM? Phase 0 risk-buster -- if anything we need for the
# hybrid handoff is unreachable here, Phase 5 must add a Program Buffer or DPI
# fallback before Phase 2 ships.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and the smallest hello.elf.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into a tickbox table at hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

# --- vsim DM transport ---------------------------------------------------------
adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

# vsim's DM uses the standard riscv DTM + DM. AX45MPV exposes one hart per core.
set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

init
halt

# --- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------------
proc probe_reg {label regno} {
    # 'reg' addresses GPRs/CSRs by symbolic name on OpenOCD; for raw regno we
    # use the abstract command directly via 'riscv dmi_read/write' helpers.
    if {[catch {set v [riscv dm_read $regno]} err]} {
        puts "MISS  $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) : $err"
        return 0
    } else {
        puts "OK    $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) = $v"
        return 1
    }
}

# --- coverage matrix -----------------------------------------------------------
puts "=== GPR x0..x31 (regno 0x1000-0x101F) ==="
set gpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set gpr_ok [expr {$gpr_ok + [probe_reg "x$i" [expr {0x1000 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "GPR reachable: $gpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== FPR f0..f31 (regno 0x1020-0x103F) ==="
set fpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set fpr_ok [expr {$fpr_ok + [probe_reg "f$i" [expr {0x1020 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "FPR reachable: $fpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== Standard M-mode CSRs (regno = csr address) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mstatus  0x300
    misa     0x301
    medeleg  0x302
    mideleg  0x303
    mie      0x304
    mtvec    0x305
    mscratch 0x340
    mepc     0x341
    mcause   0x342
    mtval    0x343
    mip      0x344
    satp     0x180
    mcycle   0xb00
    minstret 0xb02
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr CSRs) ==="
for {set i 0} {$i < 16} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpcfg$i"  [expr {0x3a0 + $i}]
}
for {set i 0} {$i < 64} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpaddr$i" [expr {0x3b0 + $i}]
}

puts "\n=== V-extension scalar CSRs ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    vstart 0x008
    vxsat  0x009
    vxrm   0x00a
    vcsr   0x00f
    vl     0xc20
    vtype  0xc21
    vlenb  0xc22
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Andes-custom CSRs (sample; full list lives in andes-v5-priv-spec) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mmsc_cfg  0xfc2
    mmisc_ctl 0x7d0
    mcache_ctl 0x7ca
    mcctlbeginaddr 0x7cb
    mcctlcommand   0x7cc
    mcctldata      0x7cd
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Vector regfile via Abstract Commands? (regno 0x1020 + offset is FPR; ==="
puts "===  V regs typically need Program Buffer load/store - testing scratch. ==="
# RISC-V Debug Spec does not assign abstract regnos to V regs; the path is
# Program Buffer. We only note the absence here; Phase 5 measures the cost.

shutdown

input.content:
# DM Abstract-Command coverage (vsim)

Phase 0 risk gate. The hybrid handoff (Phase 2+) drives architectural state
into the running RTL via the in-process JTAG/DTM and the RISC-V Debug Module
Abstract Commands. This page records which `regno` ranges actually round-trip
on each CPU variant.

If a row says **MISS**, Phase 5 must provide a Program Buffer or DPI fallback
before that field can be transferred. Phase 2 ships only after the GPR/CSR
columns marked "required" are all OK.

## How to populate this table

```bash
# in one terminal
./build/sim_ax45mpv_premium --jtag-port 9104 hybrid/tests/hello.elf

# in another terminal
openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 \
  | tee hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.log
```

Then translate the log into the table below.

## Coverage matrix

| Group              | Regnos              | Required for | ax45mpv_premium | ax66 | d45 |
| ------------------ | ------------------- | ------------ | --------------- | ---- | --- |
| GPR x0..x31        | 0x1000-0x101F       | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| FPR f0..f31        | 0x1020-0x103F       | Phase 2 (FP) | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mstatus            | 0x300               | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mepc/mtvec/mcause  | 0x341/0x305/0x342   | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mtval/mscratch     | 0x343/0x340         | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| medeleg/mideleg    | 0x302/0x303         | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mip/mie            | 0x344/0x304         | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| mcycle/minstret    | 0xB00/0xB02         | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| satp               | 0x180               | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| pmpcfg0..15        | 0x3A0-0x3AF         | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| pmpaddr0..63       | 0x3B0-0x3EF         | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| V scalar CSRs      | 0x008,0xC20-0xC22   | Phase 5 (V)  | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| V regfile v0..v31  | (no abstract regno) | Phase 5 (V)  | n/a (PB)        | -    | -   |
| Andes mmsc_cfg     | 0xFC2               | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| Andes mmisc_ctl    | 0x7D0               | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| Andes mcache_ctl   | 0x7CA               | Phase 5      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |

`PB` = Program Buffer fallback required (no abstract-regno path).

## Decision rule

- All Phase 2 rows OK -> green light to start Phase 2.
- Any Phase 2 row MISS -> file a Phase 5 ticket and either (a) add a Program
  Buffer fallback for that regno, or (b) drop the field from the v1 ABI and
  document the gap.

content.text:
# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup

Review all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.

## Phase 1: Identify Changes

Run `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.

## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel

Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.

### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review

For each change:

1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Look for similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.
2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.
3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.

### Agent 2: Code Quality Review

Review the same changes for hacky patterns:

1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls
2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones
3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction
4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries
5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase
6. **Unnecessary JSX nesting**: wrapper Boxes/elements that add no layout value — check if inner component props (flexShrink, alignItems, etc.) already provide the needed behavior
7. **Unnecessary comments**: comments explaining WHAT the code does (well-named identifiers already do that), narrating the change, or referencing the task/caller — delete; keep only non-obvious WHY (hidden constraints, subtle invariants, workarounds)

### Agent 3: Efficiency Review

Review the same changes for efficiency:

1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns
2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel
3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths
4. **Recurring no-op updates**: state/store updates inside polling loops, intervals, or event handlers that fire unconditionally — add a change-detection guard so downstream consumers aren't notified when nothing changed. Also: if a wrapper function takes an updater/reducer callback, verify it honors same-reference returns (or whatever the "no change" signal is) — otherwise callers' early-return no-ops are silently defeated
5. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error
6. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks
7. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one

## Phase 3: Fix Issues

Wait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.

When done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).
Running diff to capture context, then launching 3 review agents in parallel.

input.command: git diff CMakeLists.txt && echo "===NEW FILES===" && for f in cmake/HybridConfig.cmake hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md; do echo "===FILE: $f==="; cat "$f"; done

Code-reuse review for Phase 0 changes in /local/nick/vsim. The goal: find existing utilities/patterns in the repo that the new code duplicates or could leverage.

The new code (all you need to review):

CMakeLists.txt diff (one-liner):
```
+# Hybrid (QEMU + Verilator) - Phase 0
+include(${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake)
```

cmake/HybridConfig.cmake (new):
- Adds VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID option, VSIM_QEMU_ROOT cache var
- Calls vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
- target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include)
- add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)
- Warns if VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but VSIM_QEMU_ROOT/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h missing

hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h (new): packed C struct hybrid_state_v1 with magic/version/flags constants, GPR/FPR/CSR/V fields, includes state_abi_check.h at bottom.

hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h (new): static_assert wall on offsets and sizes of hybrid_state_v1.

tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp (new): doctest TU with 9 TEST_CASEs covering magic, layout, sizing, memcpy roundtrip. Uses #define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN.

hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl (new): OpenOCD probe of DM Abstract-Command regnos. Standalone Tcl, no codebase touchpoints.

hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md (new): manual results template.

Search the repo for reuse opportunities:

1. **Test helpers**: look at src/tests/ and tests/ for doctest patterns. Is there a shared doctest main, helper macros for offsetof checks, layout-asserting test fixtures? See tests/pipeview/kanata_logger_loopgate.test.cpp and src/tests/platform/simple_memory.test.cpp for patterns.

2. **CMake patterns**: is there an existing helper for "add a test that needs hybrid/include + nothing else"? Look at the vsim_add_test function in CMakeLists.txt:528 and see if I'm duplicating its responsibilities or if I should extend it instead. Is there an existing pattern for "include this if option X is on"?

3. **RISC-V state structs**: search the codebase for existing structs that hold GPR[32]/CSR/FPR state — could be in jtag_dtm.hpp, simulator_context.hpp, an OpenOCD adapter, or the saved-context format. If something exists, the new state_abi.h might overlap or could be aligned with it.

4. **Magic/version bytes**: search for existing 4-byte magic constants in the repo (gz_memory.hpp, simulator_context.hpp, etc.) — is there a convention I should follow? E.g. file header format with magic+version already used.

5. **Static-assert helpers**: any existing HYBRID_STATIC_ASSERT-like macro? grep for `_Static_assert` and `static_assert` pairs.

6. **OpenOCD/JTAG scripts**: is there an existing hybrid/scripts/-equivalent or tests/scripts/ directory with OpenOCD .tcl? Look at src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp comments and any companion .tcl/.cfg/.gdb files.

7. **Doc location**: is there a docs/ tree where hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md should live instead of a sibling hybrid/docs/?

Report each opportunity with: (a) where it lives in the existing codebase with file:line, (b) what the new code should change to use it. Be concrete. If you find nothing, say "no reuse opportunities found" for that category.

Keep total response under 400 words.

Code-quality review for Phase 0 changes in /local/nick/vsim. Look for hacky patterns, redundant state, sprawl, copy-paste, leaky abstractions, stringly-typed code, and unnecessary comments.

The new files (review only these):

1. **cmake/HybridConfig.cmake** — option, cache var, vsim_add_test call, add_dependencies(check ...), conditional message warning if qemu-plugin.h missing.

2. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h** — packed C struct hybrid_state_v1 (magic/version/flags/hart_id/pc/priv/gpr[32]/fpr[32]/13 M-mode CSRs/pmpcfg[16]/pmpaddr[64]/V state/vreg[32*128]/reserved[256]). #defines for HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC, HYBRID_STATE_VERSION, HYBRID_FLAG_*, HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX, HYBRID_PMP_CFG_NUM/ADDR_NUM. C-and-C++ compatible (extern "C"). Includes state_abi_check.h at the bottom unconditionally.

3. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h** — wall of HYBRID_STATIC_ASSERT(offsetof(...) == LITERAL, "msg") for every field, plus sizeof asserts. The macro picks static_assert for C++ and _Static_assert for C.

4. **tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp** — 9 doctest TEST_CASEs. Uses ((hybrid_state_v1*) 0)->field for sizeof tricks (legal but C-style; could use std::declval<hybrid_state_v1>().field). roundtrip test populates fields and memcmp's via memcpy.

5. **hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl** — OpenOCD script with hand-written probe_reg helper, three sections of foreach loops over CSR name/addr pairs.

6. **hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md** — markdown template with TBD cells.

Read each file, then evaluate for:

- **Redundant state**: e.g., is `hart_id` already implicit elsewhere? Are flag bits redundant with the struct contents (could "FP present" be derived from non-zero fpr[])?
- **Comments**: identify comments that explain WHAT (well-named identifiers do that already), or narrate the change/Phase. Examples to look for: "// Header (32 bytes)", "// = HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC", "// Phase 0.1 RED" header in test file, "Phase 0 risk gate" prose. Per the user's CLAUDE.md they want comments removed unless they explain non-obvious WHY.
- **Stringly-typed code**: in the .tcl, the foreach lists "name addr" pairs — is there a less stringly way? The static_assert macro msg "magic@0" is stringly but unavoidable.
- **Copy-paste**: state_abi.h declares 13 M-mode CSRs as 13 separate uint64_t fields; state_abi_check.h has 13 corresponding asserts; the test has separate CHECKs for some. Is there an X-macro pattern that would unify them?
- **Macro naming**: HYBRID_STATIC_ASSERT — is this needed in C++20 (where static_assert handles both)? In C11+ _Static_assert is a keyword. Could just use static_assert/_Static_assert directly with a small #define guard.
- **Test patterns**: ((hybrid_state_v1*) 0)->field is technically UB for the offsetof-via-null trick but compilers tolerate it. Cleaner alternatives?
- **Leaky abstraction**: state_abi.h includes state_abi_check.h at the BOTTOM. This means every TU that includes the ABI re-checks all asserts at every compile. Acceptable for a small struct; could break if asserts grow and become expensive.
- **CMake**: target_include_directories AFTER vsim_add_test. Is the helper expected to take include paths as arg? Should we extend vsim_add_test instead of post-tweaking?

Output format: bullet list of findings, each with file:line and a one-sentence fix suggestion. False positives are fine — flag them and let me decide. Keep under 400 words.

content.text:
## Code-Quality Review: Phase 0 Changes

**Findings (flagged by file:line):**

1. **cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:3–7** — Narrative Phase comment; remove "Phase 0: ABI scaffolding only. Adds:" boilerplate and the dash-bullet summary. Keep the comment body starting at line 14 (explains *why* always build the test).

2. **cmake/HybridConfig.cmake:14–15** — "Phase 0 always builds" and "phase of inclusion" wording dates the code and explains *when*, not *why*. Reword: "This test is always built to catch struct layout drift even in non-hybrid builds; it has no SystemC/Verilator dependency."

3. **hybrid/state_abi.h:23–24** — Comment "As the file's first four bytes when read on a little-endian host..." followed by hex literal explanation is redundant given HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC name; simplify to "0x42594841u // 'AHYB' little-endian" or omit the extra breakdown.

4. **hybrid/state_abi.h:43–45** — Comment "All fields are naturally 8-byte aligned..." explains implementation detail already evident from the code; remove if the struct design never needs re-justification. Acceptable if this is a stability note for future maintainers (keep but refocus on *why* no packed attribute is safe).

5. **hybrid/state_abi.h:47–52** — Inline comments on fields ("= HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC", "0-based hart index", etc.) explain *what* the field is; omit field-name comments unless they clarify non-obvious semantics (e.g., "priv mode at handoff" is good; "= HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC" is noise).

6. **hybrid/state_abi.h:95** — Unconditional include of state_abi_check.h at the bottom of a header means every translation unit re-runs 20+ static asserts on every compile. Acceptable for a small struct, but consider moving asserts to a separate compile-once module or a .cpp file with extern checks if asserts later become expensive.

7. **state_abi_check.h:16–18** — HYBRID_STATIC_ASSERT macro is redundant in C++20 where static_assert handles both C and C++. Modern codebases omit this. If C11 support is a hard requirement, keep it; otherwise simplify to direct static_assert/_Static_assert or a smaller #ifdef shim.

8. **tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp:1–2** — "Phase 0.1 RED: ABI contract..." and "Locks magic/version..." are narrative comments describing the test's purpose. Reword as a docstring: "Test harnesses the ABI contract: stable magic/version, byte-level roundtrip, layout locks." (Drop phase label.)

9. **tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp:43, 45, 52, 57–58** — Multiple instances of `((hybrid_state_v1*) 0)->field` for sizeof tricks. Technically UB (null dereference) but compilers fold it at compile-time. Cleaner alternatives: `std::declval<hybrid_state_v1>().field` (C++11+, avoids null) or a constexpr helper. Consider at least a code comment explaining the trick is safe here.

10. **hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl:1–11** — "Phase 0 risk-buster" and "Phase 5 must add..." are milestone-tied comments. Remove phase labels and reword as timeless design notes: "This script probes which Abstract-Command regnos work on the running vsim DM. If unreachable regnos block the handoff, a Program Buffer or DPI fallback is required before shipping."

11. **hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl:57–96** — Three foreach loops with hand-written "name addr" pairs is copy-paste-prone. Consider a single data-driven loop: `foreach reg { {mstatus 0x300} {misa 0x301} ... } { lassign $reg name addr; probe_reg $name $addr }` to reduce duplication and mistakes.

12. **hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md:3–10** — "Phase 0 risk gate", "Phase 2 ships", "Phase 5 must" are all milestone-tied prose. Decouple from versioning: reword as "Before shipping hybrid handoff, all Phase 2–required rows must be OK. If a row is MISS, add Program Buffer/DPI fallback or drop the field from the v1 ABI."

input.prompt:
Efficiency review for Phase 0 changes in /local/nick/vsim. The changes are scaffolding only (no runtime behavior in vsim's hot path) but check anyway.

New files to review:

1. **cmake/HybridConfig.cmake** — adds always-built test_state_abi to `make check`. Compiles tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp every build.
   - test_state_abi has NO dependency on SystemC/Verilator/spdlog/fmt — purely doctest + the ABI header. So compiling it is cheap.
   - But: does `add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)` slow down `make check` runtime?
   - Is the qemu-plugin.h existence check (file IO at configure time) on hot configure path?

2. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h** — single struct, included by tests today; in Phase 2+ will be included by the QEMU plugin and src/hybrid/. The struct is 5672 bytes, lives on the stack of any function that declares one. It is also memset to zero in zero-init contexts.
   - Any concern with stack pressure (5672 bytes ~= 0.6% of an 8 MB stack)?
   - The vreg[32*128] is the bulk of the struct (4 KB). Is allocating + memset + memcpy of 5672 bytes per handoff acceptable? (Phase 2+ concern, but flag now if it might be.)
   - Does state_abi.h transitively pulling in state_abi_check.h at every include site cause repeat static_assert evaluation cost across many TUs? (Compile-time cost.)

3. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h** — 25 static_asserts. Trivially cheap at compile time. Probably not a concern.

4. **tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp** — uses #define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN, which makes doctest emit its main + reporters. The roundtrip test memsets and memcpys 5672 bytes once. Trivial.
   - The roundtrip test loops `for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(src.vreg); ++i) src.vreg[i] = (uint8_t) i;` — that's 4096 byte writes per test. Could use std::iota. Negligible.

5. **hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl** — manual probe script, run by user. The probe loop hits the DM ~150 times. Each DMI round-trip in vsim costs SC cycles. Rough cost: 150 * ~50 cycles = ~7500 cycles total. At 5 KHz vsim, that's 1.5s. Acceptable for a one-shot diagnostic.
   - But: the script has no early exit if DM is unreachable. If `init` fails, all 150 probes still attempt and fill the log with errors.

6. **hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md** — markdown only, no perf concern.

Look specifically for:
- Anything in CMake that does file IO on every configure that could be guarded by VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
- Anything in the test that would cause the `make check` step to grow noticeably
- Compile-time cost of including state_abi.h transitively pulling state_abi_check.h
- Stack-size concerns when struct hybrid_state_v1 is later instantiated on the stack of vsim hot loops
- The .tcl's lack of "fail fast on init failure"

Keep response under 350 words. Flag false positives and let me decide.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to compile a comprehensive report. Let me create my final response:

## Code-Reuse Review: Phase 0 Hybrid Changes

### 1. Test Helpers: DOCTEST Pattern (REUSE OPPORTUNITY)
**Location:** `/local/nick/vsim/src/tests/platform/simple_memory.test.cpp` (line 1-5) and `/local/nick/vsim/tests/pipeview/kanata_logger_loopgate.test.cpp` (line 1-4)

**Finding:** Your `tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp` correctly follows the existing doctest pattern (`#define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN` + `#include <doctest.h>`). No duplication—you matched the house style. The offsetof/layout assertions in your test are well-structured and don't conflict with existing patterns.

**Action:** None needed. Layout validation is already best-practice in the repo (see simple_memory.test.cpp's TLM payload checks).

---

### 2. CMake Test Pattern: vsim_add_test (MINOR EXTENSION OPPORTUNITY)
**Location:** `/local/nick/vsim/CMakeLists.txt:528-541`

lastPrompt: start the phase 0 , ensure TDD dev flow

content.text:
## Efficiency Analysis Summary

**False positive flagged:** The `qemu-plugin.h` existence check at lines 25-29 of HybridConfig.cmake fires **only when VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON**—it's not on the default configure path. It's guarded and safe.

**Key findings:**

1. **CMake / `make check` runtime**: Zero concern. `add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)` is a task dependency, not a runtime operation. The test compiles once (SystemC/Verilator/spdlog/fmt all absent, so cheap) and runs for < 1ms. No build-path bloat.

2. **Compile-time cost of `state_abi.h` → `state_abi_check.h` transitive inclusion**: state_abi_check.h includes state_abi.h (line 13), creating a circular guard risk. However, the include guards prevent re-evaluation. The 25 static_asserts evaluate once per TU that includes state_abi.h. This is negligible for a single ABI header included by a handful of TUs (tests + Phase 2+ plugin/hybrid src). No runaway includes detected.

3. **Stack pressure (5672 bytes)**: Not a concern in Phase 0 (test only). Phase 2+ when the struct lives on vsim's stack: 5672 bytes is ~0.7% of a typical 8 MB stack. A single handoff per simulation run (or at low frequency) is acceptable. No issue to flag now, but document that repeated stack allocation (e.g., in a hot loop) would require heap strategy.

4. **memset/memcpy of 5672 bytes per handoff**: Line 70 of state_abi.test.cpp uses aggregate initialization (hybrid_state_v1 src{}), which zero-initializes via compiler intrinsics (no explicit memset). The roundtrip test does one memcpy each direction (lines 93, 95)—trivial cost. Phase 2+ will do the same at handoff frequency (likely 1–10 Hz, not on cycle-level hot path). Acceptable.

5. **test_dm_coverage.tcl "fail-fast" gap**: **Legitimate issue.** Lines 26–27 call `init` and `halt` without error checking. If JTAG init fails, the 150+ probes (lines 45–108) all execute anyway, flooding the log. **Recommendation**: Wrap the probe loops in a guard:
   ```tcl
   if {[catch {init} err]} {
       puts "FATAL: DM init failed: $err"
       shutdown
       exit 1
   }
   ```

**Bottom line:** Phase 0 scaffolding is clean. The tcl script needs a one-liner guard for init failure; everything else is well-designed for eventual runtime efficiency.

Efficiency review for Phase 0 changes in /local/nick/vsim. The changes are scaffolding only (no runtime behavior in vsim's hot path) but check anyway.

New files to review:

1. **cmake/HybridConfig.cmake** — adds always-built test_state_abi to `make check`. Compiles tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp every build.
   - test_state_abi has NO dependency on SystemC/Verilator/spdlog/fmt — purely doctest + the ABI header. So compiling it is cheap.
   - But: does `add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)` slow down `make check` runtime?
   - Is the qemu-plugin.h existence check (file IO at configure time) on hot configure path?

2. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h** — single struct, included by tests today; in Phase 2+ will be included by the QEMU plugin and src/hybrid/. The struct is 5672 bytes, lives on the stack of any function that declares one. It is also memset to zero in zero-init contexts.
   - Any concern with stack pressure (5672 bytes ~= 0.6% of an 8 MB stack)?
   - The vreg[32*128] is the bulk of the struct (4 KB). Is allocating + memset + memcpy of 5672 bytes per handoff acceptable? (Phase 2+ concern, but flag now if it might be.)
   - Does state_abi.h transitively pulling in state_abi_check.h at every include site cause repeat static_assert evaluation cost across many TUs? (Compile-time cost.)

3. **hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h** — 25 static_asserts. Trivially cheap at compile time. Probably not a concern.

4. **tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp** — uses #define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN, which makes doctest emit its main + reporters. The roundtrip test memsets and memcpys 5672 bytes once. Trivial.
   - The roundtrip test loops `for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(src.vreg); ++i) src.vreg[i] = (uint8_t) i;` — that's 4096 byte writes per test. Could use std::iota. Negligible.

5. **hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl** — manual probe script, run by user. The probe loop hits the DM ~150 times. Each DMI round-trip in vsim costs SC cycles. Rough cost: 150 * ~50 cycles = ~7500 cycles total. At 5 KHz vsim, that's 1.5s. Acceptable for a one-shot diagnostic.
   - But: the script has no early exit if DM is unreachable. If `init` fails, all 150 probes still attempt and fill the log with errors.

6. **hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md** — markdown only, no perf concern.

Look specifically for:
- Anything in CMake that does file IO on every configure that could be guarded by VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
- Anything in the test that would cause the `make check` step to grow noticeably
- Compile-time cost of including state_abi.h transitively pulling state_abi_check.h
- Stack-size concerns when struct hybrid_state_v1 is later instantiated on the stack of vsim hot loops
- The .tcl's lack of "fail fast on init failure"

Keep response under 350 words. Flag false positives and let me decide.

file.content:
# DM Abstract-Command coverage (vsim)

Phase 0 risk gate. The hybrid handoff (Phase 2+) drives architectural state
into the running RTL via the in-process JTAG/DTM and the RISC-V Debug Module
Abstract Commands. This page records which `regno` ranges actually round-trip
on each CPU variant.

If a row says **MISS**, Phase 5 must provide a Program Buffer or DPI fallback
before that field can be transferred. Phase 2 ships only after the GPR/CSR
columns marked "required" are all OK.

## How to populate this table

```bash
# in one terminal
./build/sim_ax45mpv_premium --jtag-port 9104 hybrid/tests/hello.elf

# in another terminal
openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 \
  | tee hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.log
```

Then translate the log into the table below.

## Coverage matrix

| Group              | Regnos              | Required for | ax45mpv_premium | ax66 | d45 |
| ------------------ | ------------------- | ------------ | --------------- | ---- | --- |
| GPR x0..x31        | 0x1000-0x101F       | Phase 2      | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |
| FPR f0..f31        | 0x1020-0x103F       | Phase 2 (FP) | TBD             | TBD  | TBD |

input.old_string:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.
#
# Phase 0: ABI scaffolding only. Adds:
#   - VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID option (default OFF; harmless when off)
#   - VSIM_QEMU_ROOT path (used by Phase 2+ to build the TCG plugin)
#   - hybrid/include on the include path for the ABI test
#   - test_state_abi doctest target

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# Phase 0 always builds the ABI test so that struct layout drift is caught
# even in non-hybrid builds. The test has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)

# Surface the hybrid test in `make check`.
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Plugin build (Phase 2+) will fail.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
endif()

input.old_string:
# OpenOCD probe: which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable
# on the running vsim DM? Phase 0 risk-buster -- if anything we need for the
# hybrid handoff is unreachable here, Phase 5 must add a Program Buffer or DPI
# fallback before Phase 2 ships.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and the smallest hello.elf.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into a tickbox table at hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

# --- vsim DM transport ---------------------------------------------------------
adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

# vsim's DM uses the standard riscv DTM + DM. AX45MPV exposes one hart per core.
set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

init
halt
input.new_string:
# Probes which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable on
# the running vsim DM. Unreachable regnos block the hybrid handoff for that
# field unless we add a Program Buffer or DPI fallback.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and a small ELF.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee /tmp/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

if {[catch {init} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: DM init failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}
if {[catch {halt} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: hart halt failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# OpenOCD probe: which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable
# on the running vsim DM? Phase 0 risk-buster -- if anything we need for the
# hybrid handoff is unreachable here, Phase 5 must add a Program Buffer or DPI
# fallback before Phase 2 ships.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and the smallest hello.elf.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into a tickbox table at hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

# --- vsim DM transport ---------------------------------------------------------
adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

# vsim's DM uses the standard riscv DTM + DM. AX45MPV exposes one hart per core.
set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

init
halt

# --- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------------
proc probe_reg {label regno} {
    # 'reg' addresses GPRs/CSRs by symbolic name on OpenOCD; for raw regno we
    # use the abstract command directly via 'riscv dmi_read/write' helpers.
    if {[catch {set v [riscv dm_read $regno]} err]} {
        puts "MISS  $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) : $err"
        return 0
    } else {
        puts "OK    $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) = $v"
        return 1
    }
}

# --- coverage matrix -----------------------------------------------------------
puts "=== GPR x0..x31 (regno 0x1000-0x101F) ==="
set gpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set gpr_ok [expr {$gpr_ok + [probe_reg "x$i" [expr {0x1000 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "GPR reachable: $gpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== FPR f0..f31 (regno 0x1020-0x103F) ==="
set fpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set fpr_ok [expr {$fpr_ok + [probe_reg "f$i" [expr {0x1020 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "FPR reachable: $fpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== Standard M-mode CSRs (regno = csr address) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mstatus  0x300
    misa     0x301
    medeleg  0x302
    mideleg  0x303
    mie      0x304
    mtvec    0x305
    mscratch 0x340
    mepc     0x341
    mcause   0x342
    mtval    0x343
    mip      0x344
    satp     0x180
    mcycle   0xb00
    minstret 0xb02
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr CSRs) ==="
for {set i 0} {$i < 16} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpcfg$i"  [expr {0x3a0 + $i}]
}
for {set i 0} {$i < 64} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpaddr$i" [expr {0x3b0 + $i}]
}

puts "\n=== V-extension scalar CSRs ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    vstart 0x008
    vxsat  0x009
    vxrm   0x00a
    vcsr   0x00f
    vl     0xc20
    vtype  0xc21
    vlenb  0xc22
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Andes-custom CSRs (sample; full list lives in andes-v5-priv-spec) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mmsc_cfg  0xfc2
    mmisc_ctl 0x7d0
    mcache_ctl 0x7ca
    mcctlbeginaddr 0x7cb
    mcctlcommand   0x7cc
    mcctldata      0x7cd
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Vector regfile via Abstract Commands? (regno 0x1020 + offset is FPR; ==="
puts "===  V regs typically need Program Buffer load/store - testing scratch. ==="
# RISC-V Debug Spec does not assign abstract regnos to V regs; the path is
# Program Buffer. We only note the absence here; Phase 5 measures the cost.

shutdown

- Stripped phase-tied narrative ("Phase 0", "Phase 2 must...") from CMakeLists, ABI header, check header, test, .tcl, and doc

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested building a hybrid simulator that combines Verilator-based vsim (cycle-accurate, ~5-9 KHz) with QEMU (fast, MHz). The hybrid runs uninteresting code (boot/libc init) in QEMU, switches to Verilator for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), and optionally hands back. Specific requests in chronological order:
   - "make a plan to build a hybrid simulator with verilator (vsim) and QEMU... Write a tutorial to introduce how this hybrid mechanism is technically feasible. and the complete plan/steps."
   - Write the plan into `hybrid_plan.md`
   - "start the phase 0, ensure TDD dev flow"
   - Run `/simplify` code review
   - "pass all tests?" (verification check)
   
   The biggest technical challenge identified: bidirectional CPU state exchange (registers + memory) between QEMU and Verilator.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Verilator + SystemC simulation (vsim project)
   - QEMU TCG plugin API (qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb, qemu_plugin_read_register)
   - RISC-V Debug Module Abstract Commands (regnos: GPR 0x1000-0x101F, FPR 0x1020-0x103F, CSRs 0x000-0xFFF)
   - JTAG/DTM in-process driving (no socket)
   - Shared mmap RAM via `memory-backend-file,share=on`
   - Magic CSR 0x7C0 trigger convention (csrwi 0x7C0, 0=enter, 1=exit)
   - TDD discipline: RED → GREEN → REFACTOR with structural/behavioral commit prefixes
   - doctest framework
   - C/C++ dual-language ABI headers
   - Static_assert layout locking
   - Andes RISC-V CPU variants (AX45MPV, AX66, etc.)
   - Existing kanata-loop-window mechanism for ROI identification

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `/home/nick/.claude/plans/make-a-plan-to-abstract-teacup.md` (also copied to `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid_plan.md`)
     - The approved 6-phase plan covering Phase 0 (plumbing/ABI), Phase 1 (mmap RAM), Phase 2 (QEMU→vsim handoff), Phase 3 (round-trip), Phase 4 (kanata integration), Phase 5 (hardening)
     - Locked design decisions: JTAG/DTM in-process for register write-back, mmap shared RAM, packed C struct ABI, TCG plugin against QEMU 9.2.0 at `/local/nick/qemu_v5`

   - `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h` (created, then simplified)
     - Wire contract between QEMU plugin and vsim
     - 5672-byte struct hybrid_state_v1
     - Final simplified version:
     ```c
     #ifndef HYBRID_STATE_ABI_H
     #define HYBRID_STATE_ABI_H
     #include <stdint.h>
     #include <stddef.h>
     #ifdef __cplusplus
     extern "C" {
     #endif
     #define HYBRID_STATE_MAGIC   0x42594841u  // 'AHYB' little-endian
     #define HYBRID_STATE_VERSION 1u
     #define HYBRID_FLAG_RV64 (1u << 0)
     #define HYBRID_FLAG_FP   (1u << 1)
     #define HYBRID_FLAG_V    (1u << 2)
     #define HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX 128
     #define HYBRID_PMP_CFG_NUM  16
     #define HYBRID_PMP_ADDR_NUM 64
     struct hybrid_state_v1 {
       uint32_t magic;
       uint32_t version;
       uint32_t flags;
       uint32_t hart_id;
       uint64_t pc;
       uint64_t priv;  // 0=U, 1=S, 3=M
       uint64_t gpr[32];
       uint64_t fpr[32];
       uint64_t mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch;
       uint64_t medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp;
       uint64_t pmpcfg[HYBRID_PMP_CFG_NUM];
       uint64_t pmpaddr[HYBRID_PMP_ADDR_NUM];
       uint64_t vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb;
       uint8_t  vreg[32 * HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX];
       uint8_t  reserved[256];
     };
     #ifdef __cplusplus
     }
     #endif
     #include "hybrid/state_abi_check.h"
     #endif
     ```

   - `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi_check.h` (created, then simplified)
     - 28 static_assert offset/size locks
     - Final simplified version uses direct `static_assert`/`_Static_assert` (no HYBRID_STATIC_ASSERT macro)
     - Sample asserts: magic@0, version@4, flags@8, hart_id@12, pc@16, priv@24, gpr@32, fpr@288, mstatus@544...satp@640, pmpcfg@648, pmpaddr@776, vstart@1288, vreg@1320, reserved@5416, total size 5672

   - `/local/nick/vsim/tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp` (created, then refined)
     - 9 doctest TEST_CASEs, 104 assertions
     - Uses #define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN
     - Replaced UB null-deref `((T*)0)->field` trick with stack instances
     - Tests: magic/version, struct properties (standard-layout, trivially copyable), header offsets, GPR/FPR layout, V-extension MAX_VLEN, PMP arrays, reserved padding, byte-level memcpy roundtrip, flag bits

   - `/local/nick/vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (created, then simplified)
     - Final version:
     ```cmake
     option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)
     set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
       CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")
     vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
     target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
       "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
     )
     add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)
     if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
       if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
         message(WARNING "...")
       endif()
       message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
     endif()
     ```

   - `/local/nick/vsim/CMakeLists.txt` (modified)
     - Added one-line include after test_kanata_logger_loopgate target setup:
     ```cmake
     # Hybrid (QEMU + Verilator) - Phase 0
     include(${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake)
     ```

   - `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl` (created, then refined)
     - OpenOCD probe script for DM Abstract-Command regno reachability
     - Probes GPR x0..x31, FPR f0..f31, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V scalar CSRs, Andes-custom CSRs
     - After review: added fail-fast `catch` guards around `init` and `halt`

   - `/local/nick/vsim/docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md` (moved from hybrid/docs/)
     - Markdown table template with TBD cells for ax45mpv_premium/ax66/d45 columns
     - Decision rule: any required-row MISS → add Program Buffer/DPI fallback or drop field

4. Errors and fixes:

   - **clang++ failed to compile doctest** with errors about `std::mutex`, `SA_ONSTACK`, IReporter casts:
     - Cause: host clang is from riscv toolchain `/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...` with libc++ stdlib path issues
     - Fix: switched to `g++` for standalone compilation. Tests passed 9/9.
   
   - **CMake reconfigure failed** with "current CMakeCache.txt directory is different than the directory /work where CMakeCache.txt was created":
     - Cause: existing build/ dir was from Docker container build at /work
     - Fix: tried fresh `build-hybrid/` dir but default `clang` is riscv toolchain. Cleaned up and verified test compilation standalone with g++ instead. Full Docker build not attempted in session.

   - **`git mv` failed** for hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md → docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md:
     - Cause: source file was untracked
     - Fix: used plain `mv` then `rmdir` of empty hybrid/docs/

   - **Plan mode interaction**: User rejected initial ExitPlanMode after Ultraplan was attempted; later user instructed login then write plan to hybrid_plan.md
     - Fix: Called ExitPlanMode again (approved), then copied plan to project hybrid_plan.md

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Solved**: ABI design with versioned magic, exact byte layout, packed without `__attribute__((packed))` (natural 8-byte alignment), C/C++ dual-language compatibility

# Probes which RISC-V Debug Module Abstract-Command regnos are reachable on
# the running vsim DM. Unreachable regnos block the hybrid handoff for that
# field unless we add a Program Buffer or DPI fallback.
#
# Usage:
#   1. Start vsim with --jtag-port 9104 and a small ELF.
#   2. openocd -f hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl 2>&1 | tee /tmp/dm_coverage.log
#   3. Translate the log into docs/HYBRID_DM_COVERAGE.md.
#
# Spec ref: RISC-V External Debug Support 1.0, table "Abstract Register Numbers".

adapter driver remote_bitbang
remote_bitbang_host localhost
remote_bitbang_port 9104

set _CHIPNAME riscv
jtag newtap $_CHIPNAME cpu -irlen 5 -expected-id 0x10e31913
set _TARGETNAME $_CHIPNAME.cpu
target create $_TARGETNAME riscv -chain-position $_TARGETNAME

riscv set_command_timeout_sec 5

if {[catch {init} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: DM init failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}
if {[catch {halt} err]} {
    puts "FATAL: hart halt failed: $err"
    shutdown error
}

# --- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------------
proc probe_reg {label regno} {
    # 'reg' addresses GPRs/CSRs by symbolic name on OpenOCD; for raw regno we
    # use the abstract command directly via 'riscv dmi_read/write' helpers.
    if {[catch {set v [riscv dm_read $regno]} err]} {
        puts "MISS  $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) : $err"
        return 0
    } else {
        puts "OK    $label (regno=[format 0x%04x $regno]) = $v"
        return 1
    }
}

# --- coverage matrix -----------------------------------------------------------
puts "=== GPR x0..x31 (regno 0x1000-0x101F) ==="
set gpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set gpr_ok [expr {$gpr_ok + [probe_reg "x$i" [expr {0x1000 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "GPR reachable: $gpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== FPR f0..f31 (regno 0x1020-0x103F) ==="
set fpr_ok 0
for {set i 0} {$i < 32} {incr i} {
    set fpr_ok [expr {$fpr_ok + [probe_reg "f$i" [expr {0x1020 + $i}]]}]
}
puts "FPR reachable: $fpr_ok / 32"

puts "\n=== Standard M-mode CSRs (regno = csr address) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mstatus  0x300
    misa     0x301
    medeleg  0x302
    mideleg  0x303
    mie      0x304
    mtvec    0x305
    mscratch 0x340
    mepc     0x341
    mcause   0x342
    mtval    0x343
    mip      0x344
    satp     0x180
    mcycle   0xb00
    minstret 0xb02
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr CSRs) ==="
for {set i 0} {$i < 16} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpcfg$i"  [expr {0x3a0 + $i}]
}
for {set i 0} {$i < 64} {incr i} {
    probe_reg "pmpaddr$i" [expr {0x3b0 + $i}]
}

puts "\n=== V-extension scalar CSRs ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    vstart 0x008
    vxsat  0x009
    vxrm   0x00a
    vcsr   0x00f
    vl     0xc20
    vtype  0xc21
    vlenb  0xc22
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Andes-custom CSRs (sample; full list lives in andes-v5-priv-spec) ==="
foreach {name addr} {
    mmsc_cfg  0xfc2
    mmisc_ctl 0x7d0
    mcache_ctl 0x7ca
    mcctlbeginaddr 0x7cb
    mcctlcommand   0x7cc
    mcctldata      0x7cd
} {
    probe_reg $name $addr
}

puts "\n=== Vector regfile via Abstract Commands? (regno 0x1020 + offset is FPR; ==="
puts "===  V regs typically need Program Buffer load/store - testing scratch. ==="
# RISC-V Debug Spec does not assign abstract regnos to V regs; the path is
# Program Buffer. We only note the absence here; Phase 5 measures the cost.

shutdown

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. The TCG plugin build will fail.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
endif()

attachment.planContent:
# Hybrid Simulator: QEMU + Verilator (vsim)

A plan to extend `vsim` from a pure Verilator cycle-accurate simulator into a hybrid functional/RTL simulator that runs the boring code in QEMU at MHz speed and switches into Verilator only for the cycle-accurate region of interest (ROI), then optionally hands back.

---

## 1. Context

`vsim` runs at 5-9 KHz on Andes RISC-V cores. A typical workload spends >99% of cycles in boot, libc init, and warm-up before reaching the kernel under study. Today the only escape hatch is `--save`/`--continue`, which still requires an initial RTL run to reach the snapshot point. There is also already a `--kanata-loop-{start,end,skip,record}` mechanism (`src/main.cpp:183-214`) that identifies the interesting loop iterations.

We want: run everything before the ROI in QEMU (functional, MHz), hand the architectural state to Verilator at the ROI boundary, run the ROI cycle-accurately to record kanata/clarity pipeline data, and optionally hand back to QEMU to finish.

The intended outcome: 100x-1000x reduction in wall-clock time for benchmarking workflows that only care about steady-state pipeline behavior of a small kernel, with byte-identical kanata output for the recorded window vs. a fully-RTL baseline.

---

## 2. Tutorial: Why This Is Technically Feasible

### 2.1 The state-exchange problem, reframed

A naive hybrid sim has to "transfer" CPU state between two simulators. In practice the state splits into three categories with very different costs:

| Category | Items | Size | Strategy |
|---|---|---|---|
| Architectural regs | x0..x31, f0..f31, V regs, key CSRs | 4-8 KB | **Copy** via well-defined ABI struct |
| Memory | guest RAM, ILM/DLM | MB-GB | **Share** via `mmap` - zero copy |
| Microarchitectural state | TLB, caches, BPU, store buffer | KB-MB | **Discard** - let cold starts repopulate |

The key trick: **memory does not move**. QEMU runs with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=/dev/shm/vsim-ram`. vsim's `SimpleMemory::data` (`src/platform/simple_memory.hpp:20`) is changed to an `mmap` of the same fd. Both simulators read and write the same physical bytes. The same trick is used by QEMU's CPR (`docs/devel/migration/CPR.rst`) and gem5's `SimpleSwitchableProcessor`. RAM size becomes irrelevant.

Microarch state is intentionally discarded. We accept cold caches/BPU on entry into Verilator and rely on the existing `kanata_loop_skip N` mechanism to warm them up before recording. This matches the gem5 "switch + warmup" pattern.

### 2.2 The trigger: a magic CSR convention

We need both sides to agree on "the ROI starts here." Reuse the user-defined CSR space at `0x7C0`:

```asm
csrwi 0x7C0, 0   # enter detail (QEMU -> vsim)
csrwi 0x7C0, 1   # exit detail  (vsim -> QEMU)
```

- **QEMU** sees this via a TCG plugin: `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()` with a per-instruction filter that pattern-matches the encoding of `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` (mask `0xfff03073` against the 32-bit insn). On hit, the plugin drains state and exits.
- **vsim** sees this via the existing retire signal taps in `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`. We pre-scan the ELF for PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *` (LIEF is already linked - `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp`), then watch for retire-of-PC against that set.

Phase 4 lifts this further: `--kanata-loop-start <pc>` and `--kanata-loop-end <pc>` already specify the ROI by PC. The orchestrator wraps these as PC-trigger events and **the binary needs no magic CSR at all**.

We deliberately avoid `tohost`/`fromhost` (HTIF reserved) and `ecall` (already used by the runtime).

### 2.3 The write-back path: existing JTAG/DTM, in-process

This is the highest-leverage finding. vsim already has:

- `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` - a full JTAG DTM implementation with the RISC-V Debug Module
- `--jtag-port` CLI flag and OpenOCD support (`src/main.cpp:148-154`)

The RISC-V Debug Spec gives Abstract Commands that read/write GPRs (`regno` 0x1000-0x101F), FPRs (0x1020-0x103F), and CSRs (0x0000-0x0FFF) directly via the Debug Module. **Writing all 32 GPRs + 32 FPRs + ~20 critical CSRs is ~85 DMI transactions**, which is ~hundreds of cycles - negligible compared to the millions of cycles we save.

We drive the DTM **in-process**, calling its `handle_dmi_*` methods directly without an OpenOCD socket. No DPI shim, no Verilog modifications, no per-CPU-variant code (works for all ten CPU wrappers in `src/cpu_wrappers/`).

The sequence to "resume from QEMU state":

1. Reset CPU; halt request via DM
2. For each register in the ABI struct, issue Abstract Command (`regno` + data)
3. Set DPC = saved PC
4. Issue `dret` via Program Buffer

Vector registers and any CPU-specific custom CSR that DM doesn't expose are deferred to Phase 5. If DM coverage is incomplete we add a per-CPU Program Buffer fallback or, last resort, an mmap-shared V regfile via DPI.

### 2.4 The QEMU side: a TCG plugin against the existing build

`/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` is QEMU **9.2.0** (verified). This is recent enough for the full plugin API:

- `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()` - per-insn callback
- `qemu_plugin_get_registers()` / `qemu_plugin_read_register()` - drain GPR/FP/CSR
- `qemu_plugin_request_exit()` - stop QEMU cleanly

Templates already on disk: `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/{execlog.c, hotblocks.c, bbv.c}`. We add one new plugin `hybrid_handoff.c` next to them. No QEMU patches, no fork.

### 2.5 What does NOT work and is out of scope

- **MMIO inside the ROI**: device state lives in QEMU C and has no mirror in RTL. Document; fail loudly via an interconnect watch in Phase 5.
- **lr/sc reservations across handoff**: invalidate at handoff; do not transfer.
- **Self-modifying code in the ROI**: the magic CSR is a synchronization boundary by convention.
- **mcycle/minstret continuity**: monotonic across handoffs but not equal to a fully-RTL run; documented discontinuity.
- **Andes-internal QEMU fork** (`andescycle-dev-v0.16-17-g03dfe23.tar.gz`): treated as opaque. Plan against mainline QEMU 9.2.

---

## 3. Architecture at a Glance

```
                              shared mmap RAM (/dev/shm/vsim-ram)
                                           |
              +----------------------------+----------------------------+
              |                                                         |
              v                                                         v
       QEMU 9.2.0 + plugin                                    vsim (SystemC + Verilator)
       +---------------------+                                +-----------------------+
       | TCG insn loop       |     state.bin (ABI struct)     | SystemC main loop     |
       | hybrid_handoff.so   |  <-->  [ABI v1 packed]   <-->  | jtag_dtm in-process   |
       | csrw 0x7C0 -> drain |                                | csr_trigger -> drain  |
       +---------------------+                                +-----------------------+
              ^                                                         ^
              |                                                         |
              +--orchestrator script (vsim-hybrid)----------------------+
                  enter-pc / exit-pc mode also supported
```

State exchange contract: a single packed `struct hybrid_state_v1` (header + GPR + FPR + CSR + V) defined in `hybrid/include/state_abi.h`, written atomically (`O_TMPFILE` + `linkat`) at handoff.

---

## 4. Design Decisions (locked)

| # | Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| a | **JTAG/DTM in-process** for register write-back (vs. DPI shim or synthetic insn stream) | Existing infrastructure (`src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`); zero RTL changes; works across all 10 CPU wrappers |
| b | Same for CSRs; Program Buffer fallback for unreachable ones | Same |
| c | **Modify `SimpleMemory` in-place**: backing becomes `std::span<uint8_t>` with optional mmap; existing `std::vector` path retained when mmap unset | All call sites use `.data()`/`.size()` only - both span and vector satisfy. Templated class used as concrete member; forking is worse |
| d | **TCG plugin** (in-process, fastest) against existing QEMU 9.2.0 build at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` | Plugin API is feature-complete in 9.x; templates available; no fork |
| e | **Packed C struct** in shared file (vs. protobuf/JSON) | C-and-C++-compatible single header; zero parse cost; static_assert offsets to catch drift |

---

## 5. Phased Implementation Plan

Each phase lands a working subset and is independently testable.

### Phase 0 - Plumbing, validation, ABI freeze

**Goal**: skeleton in place, riskiest assumptions de-risked, no runtime behavior change.

Deliverables:
- `hybrid/include/state_abi.h` - versioned packed struct: magic `'AHYB'`, version, flags, pc, priv, gpr[32], fpr[32], M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp, pmpcfg[16], pmpaddr[64]), V state (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[32 * MAX_VLEN_BYTES]), 256B trailing reserved.
- `hybrid/include/state_abi_check.h` - `static_assert` on offsets and size.
- `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - adds `VSIM_QEMU_ROOT` cache var (default `/local/nick/qemu_v5`), build option `VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`.
- `hybrid/scripts/test_dm_coverage.tcl` - 50-line OpenOCD script connecting to vsim's DM, probing which `regno` ranges return success. **De-risks the JTAG decision.** Document output in `hybrid/docs/dm_coverage.md`.

Test: vsim builds with `-DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON`, plugin builds against `/local/nick/qemu_v5`, struct `static_assert`s pass.

Risk to escalate: if DM Abstract Commands cannot reach vector regs / Andes-custom CSRs, Phase 5 must add Program Buffer or DPI - decide before starting Phase 2.

### Phase 1 - Shared mmap RAM

**Goal**: vsim runs identically to today, but main memory is mmap-backed when `--shared-mem-path` is set.

Files modified:
- `src/platform/simple_memory.hpp:20` - `std::vector<uint8_t> data` becomes a `std::span<uint8_t> data` plus an optional owning `std::vector<uint8_t> owned_storage_` plus a `bool owns_mapping_` flag. Add a constructor taking `(int fd, size_t offset, size_t size)` that mmaps in.
- `src/platform/platform.hpp` - thread mmap fd from `Simulator` into `main_memory` construction via a `std::optional<MmapBacking>` member.
- `src/main.cpp` - new flags `--shared-mem-path <file>`, `--shared-mem-size <bytes>`. When unset, behave as today.

Files NOT modified (already span-compatible): `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp`, `src/simulator_context.hpp` (uses `.data()`/`.size()` only), `src/simulator.hpp:190,228-230`.

Test: `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.cpp` - subprocess launches `vsim --shared-mem-path /dev/shm/test_ram --shared-mem-size 256M small_loop.elf`, parent process mmaps the same file, verifies `.text` bytes match objdump and that an in-program store is visible to the parent.

### Phase 2 - One-way handoff: QEMU -> vsim

**Goal**: program runs under QEMU, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`, QEMU drains state and exits. vsim is invoked with `--resume-from-qemu <state.bin>` and runs from the resumed PC to program end.

New files:
- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - modeled on `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/execlog.c`. TB callback walks insns; per-insn callback registered only on `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` matches. On fire, dispatch on `rs1` value (0=enter, 1=exit), drain GPR/FP/CSR via `qemu_plugin_read_register()`, write packed struct atomically, call `qemu_plugin_request_exit()`.
- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/meson.build` - builds `libhybrid_handoff.so`.
- `src/hybrid/qemu_resume.hpp` - reads ABI struct, validates magic/version, drives DM via `jtag_dtm` directly (no socket): halt -> for each reg issue Abstract Command -> set DPC -> `dret`.

Files modified:
- `src/main.cpp` - new flag `--resume-from-qemu <file>`, mutually exclusive with `--continue`.
- `src/simulator.hpp:239` - `reset()` extended with "resume from external state" branch that runs after `resetn` is asserted; reuses the existing JTAG infrastructure in-process.

Test: tiny RV64 program:
```asm
_start:
  csrwi 0x7C0, 0
  li a0, 1; li a1, 2; add a2, a0, a1
  li a7, 93; ecall
```
Compile, run under QEMU+plugin, observe state file. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state.bin`, verify `a2 == 3` at retire and exit code 0.

### Phase 3 - Round-trip: vsim -> QEMU

**Goal**: vsim detects exit-detail magic CSR write, drains state, hands back to QEMU on the same shared RAM.

New files:
- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - `CsrTrigger` class. ELF pre-scan computes the set of PCs containing `csrw 0x7C0, *`. Subscribes to retire signals (`wb_i0_pc`/`wb_i0_retire` from `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp`). On retire-of-PC in the set, decode the saved insn to get `rs1` register, read GPR via existing probe (`gpr_index_hart0`/`gpr_value_hart0` in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-92`). If value=1, signal handoff.
- `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - dual of `qemu_resume.hpp`: halt CPU, read all archregs via DM Abstract Commands, populate ABI struct, msync to file.

Files modified:
- `src/simulator.hpp:289-340` (the `run()` loop) - add `csr_trigger.tick(cpu)` after the per-cycle checks. On drain signal, break loop with new exit code `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU = 200`.
- `src/main.cpp` - on `EXIT_HANDOFF_TO_QEMU` plus `--qemu-binary` set, after `sim.cpu.final()` (line 342), `execve` QEMU with `-object memory-backend-file,share=on,mem-path=<shared-mem-path>` and `-plugin libhybrid_handoff.so,resume_from=<state.bin>`. The plugin's `resume_from` mode pokes state back via `qemu_plugin_write_register()` at the first TB callback.

Test: extend Phase 2 program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` after the body. Run vsim with `--resume-from-qemu state_in.bin --qemu-binary /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64`. Verify the QEMU side processes the ecall and returns 0.

### Phase 4 - Kanata-loop integration

**Goal**: existing `--kanata-loop-start <pc> --kanata-loop-end <pc>` flags drive hybrid mode automatically. **No magic CSR required in the binary.**

New files:
- `hybrid/scripts/vsim-hybrid` (Python orchestrator):
  1. Inspect ELF + user's loop PCs.
  2. Launch QEMU with hybrid plugin in **PC-trigger mode** (`enter_pc=<start>`, `exit_pc=<end>` plugin args).
  3. On QEMU exit, launch vsim in resume mode with state file + the kanata flags unchanged.
  4. On vsim's exit-detail, optionally re-launch QEMU to finish.

Files modified:
- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - PC-trigger mode added; cheap with `qemu_plugin_register_vcpu_insn_exec_cb()`.
- `src/hybrid/csr_trigger.hpp` - sibling `PcTrigger` class for `--hybrid-enter-pc` / `--hybrid-exit-pc` CLI flags.
- `src/main.cpp` - `--hybrid-mode=auto` aliases `--kanata-loop-start/end` to the hybrid PC triggers.

Test: boot a benchmark from `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, hand off at the steady-state inner loop, record kanata for N iterations in vsim, hand back to QEMU. The recorded kanata file for the captured iterations must be **byte-identical** to a fully-vsim baseline run for the same window (after `kanata_loop_skip` warmup).

### Phase 5 - Hardening

Each item independently scoped:
- **Vector regfile transfer**: measure DM cost; if >100ms per handoff, add mmap-shared V regfile via DPI (mirror the `set_ilm()` pattern in `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:393-441`).
- **FP regs and `mstatus.FS`**: standard handling.
- **PMP**: all 64 pmpaddr/pmpcfg via Abstract Commands.
- **Priv-mode transitions**: set DCSR.prv so `dret` resumes in the right mode.
- **Traps inside ROI**: trap stays in vsim until exit-CSR retires.
- **MMIO touch in ROI**: add an interconnect watch (`src/platform/interconnect.hpp`), fail loudly on non-RAM addresses during the ROI window.
- **mcycle/minstret discontinuity**: sum across handoffs to keep monotonic; document the gap vs. fully-RTL.
- **lr/sc reservations**: explicitly invalidate via DCSR after resume.
- **Multi-hart**: ABI struct already carries per-hart arrays; `_NDS_NHART <= 2` already enforced (`src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:37-39`).

Test: torture workload running interrupt-driven code, V+FP active, traps inside ROI, verifying mcycle/minstret monotonic across multiple handoffs.

---

## 6. Critical Files

To modify:
- `src/platform/simple_memory.hpp` - mmap-backed storage (Phase 1)
- `src/platform/platform.hpp` - thread the mmap fd (Phase 1)
- `src/main.cpp` - new CLI flags (Phases 1-4)
- `src/simulator.hpp` - resume-from-qemu hook in `reset()`, csr_trigger tick in `run()` (Phases 2-3)
- `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - new build option (Phase 0)

To reuse without modification:
- `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` - in-process DM driver
- `src/signals/common_signals_45.hpp` - retire signal taps
- `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp:91-100` - GPR/PC probes
- `src/utils/elf_loader.hpp` - LIEF-based ELF pre-scan for trigger PCs
- `src/main.cpp:183-214` - existing `--kanata-loop-*` flag parsing

To create:
- `hybrid/include/state_abi.h`
- `hybrid/include/state_abi_check.h`
- `hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` + `meson.build`
- `hybrid/scripts/{test_dm_coverage.tcl, vsim-hybrid}`
- `src/hybrid/{qemu_resume.hpp, state_drain.hpp, csr_trigger.hpp}`

External, reused as-is:
- `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0)
- `/local/nick/qemu_v5/contrib/plugins/execlog.c` (template)

---

## 7. Verification

End-to-end ladder, each level requires the previous:

1. **Phase 0 build smoke**: `cmake -DVSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON .. && make` succeeds; plugin builds; static_asserts pass.
2. **Phase 1 mmap parity**: `tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.cpp` passes - external mmap reader sees identical bytes to vsim's view.
3. **Phase 2 one-way correctness**: tiny program ends with `a2==3` and exit 0 after handoff.
4. **Phase 3 round-trip**: tiny program with `csrwi 0x7C0, 1; ecall` exits 0; intermediate state file is consumed and discarded.
5. **Phase 4 kanata equivalence**: for a benchmark in `external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/`, the kanata file produced by hybrid mode (with appropriate `kanata_loop_skip` warmup) is byte-identical to the fully-RTL baseline for the same loop window.
6. **Phase 5 torture**: traps + V + FP + multi-handoff, mcycle monotonic, no MMIO violations.

Speed measurement at each phase: capture wall-clock for a representative workload (e.g., dhrystone, an existing testbench in `external/`). Target after Phase 4: **>=100x** speedup on workloads where the ROI is <1% of dynamic instructions.

Each phase commit follows the project's TDD discipline (see `~/personal_git/.claude/TDD.md`): a failing test first, then the smallest code to pass, then refactor. Commits prefixed `structural:` or `behavioral:`.

skills.name: simplify
skills.content:
# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup

Review all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.

## Phase 1: Identify Changes

Run `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.

## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel

Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.

### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review

For each change:

1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Look for similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.
2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.
3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.

### Agent 2: Code Quality Review

Review the same changes for hacky patterns:

1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls
2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones
3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction
4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries
5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase
6. **Unnecessary JSX nesting**: wrapper Boxes/elements that add no layout value — check if inner component props (flexShrink, alignItems, etc.) already provide the needed behavior
7. **Unnecessary comments**: comments explaining WHAT the code does (well-named identifiers already do that), narrating the change, or referencing the task/caller — delete; keep only non-obvious WHY (hidden constraints, subtle invariants, workarounds)

### Agent 3: Efficiency Review

Review the same changes for efficiency:

1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns
2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel
3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths
4. **Recurring no-op updates**: state/store updates inside polling loops, intervals, or event handlers that fire unconditionally — add a change-detection guard so downstream consumers aren't notified when nothing changed. Also: if a wrapper function takes an updater/reducer callback, verify it honors same-reference returns (or whatever the "no change" signal is) — otherwise callers' early-return no-ops are silently defeated
5. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error
6. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks
7. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one

## Phase 3: Fix Issues

Wait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.

When done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).

function(vsim_add_test TARGET SOURCE)
  add_executable(${TARGET} EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL ${SOURCE})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE ${VSIM_INCLUDE_DIRS})
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src"
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/src/utils")
  target_include_directories(${TARGET} SYSTEM PRIVATE
    "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/external/doctest/doctest")
  target_compile_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_FLAGS})
  target_compile_definitions(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_COMPILE_DEFS})
  target_link_options(${TARGET} PRIVATE ${VSIM_LINK_FLAGS})
  add_test(NAME ${TARGET} COMMAND $<TARGET_FILE:${TARGET}>)
endfunction()

1	#!/bin/bash
2	
3	# VSim Workspace Build Script
4	# This script builds released vsim image tar file
5	
6	set -e
7	
8	# arguments to docker targets
9	DOCKER_TARGET="$1"
10	if [[ ! "$DOCKER_TARGET" =~ ^(dev|runtime|bin|debug)$ ]]; then
11	    echo "Error: Invalid argument '$1'"
12	    echo "Usage: $0 {dev|runtime|bin|debug}"
13	    echo "  dev     - Development environment"
14	    echo "  runtime - Runtime environment"
15	    echo "  bin     - Binary only"
16	    echo "  debug   - debug version"
17	    exit 1
18	fi
19	
20	# Generate a version file
21	tag=$(git describe --tags --always)
22	echo "vsim version : " "$tag"
23	
24	if [[ "$DOCKER_TARGET" == "bin" ]]; then
25	    podman build --build-arg VERSION="$tag" --output=type=local,dest=andescycle-bin-"$tag" .
26	else
27	    # build image using docker or podman, archive build results
28	    podman build --target "$DOCKER_TARGET" --build-arg VERSION="$tag" -t andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" .
29	    podman save localhost/andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" | gzip > andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET"-"$tag".tar.gz
30	fi
31	
32	
33	echo "✅ Build complete"
34

file.content:
#!/bin/bash

# VSim Workspace Build Script
# This script builds released vsim image tar file

set -e

# arguments to docker targets
DOCKER_TARGET="$1"
if [[ ! "$DOCKER_TARGET" =~ ^(dev|runtime|bin|debug)$ ]]; then
    echo "Error: Invalid argument '$1'"
    echo "Usage: $0 {dev|runtime|bin|debug}"
    echo "  dev     - Development environment"
    echo "  runtime - Runtime environment"
    echo "  bin     - Binary only"
    echo "  debug   - debug version"
    exit 1
fi

# Generate a version file
tag=$(git describe --tags --always)
echo "vsim version : " "$tag"

if [[ "$DOCKER_TARGET" == "bin" ]]; then
    podman build --build-arg VERSION="$tag" --output=type=local,dest=andescycle-bin-"$tag" .
else
    # build image using docker or podman, archive build results
    podman build --target "$DOCKER_TARGET" --build-arg VERSION="$tag" -t andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" .
    podman save localhost/andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" | gzip > andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET"-"$tag".tar.gz
fi


echo "✅ Build complete"

content.subject: Phase 1.4 CLI flags + Platform plumbing
content.description: Wire `--shared-mem-path <file>` and `--shared-mem-size <bytes>` through main.cpp into Platform construction. When unset, behavior matches today (owning vector). When set, mmap RAM from the shared file.

content.subject: Phase 1.5 end-to-end shared-mem smoke test
content.description: tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.cpp - subprocess vsim with shared-mem flags; parent mmaps the same file; verify ELF .text bytes match objdump and an in-program store is visible to the parent.

content.subject: Phase 1.2 RED: failing test for mmap-backed SimpleMemory
content.description: Add a new unit test that constructs SimpleMemory with an mmap-backed buffer (open + ftruncate + mmap a tmpfile), drives a TLM write through the socket, then verifies the bytes are visible via an independent mmap of the same fd. Must fail until the new constructor exists.

content.subject: Phase 1.3 GREEN: SimpleMemory mmap constructor
content.description: Add SimpleMemory constructor accepting an mmap span (or fd+offset+size); set `data` span to the mapping; do not own a vector. Smallest implementation to make the test pass.

content.subject: Phase 1.1 structural: SimpleMemory data → span + owned vector
content.description: Refactor `data` from `std::vector<uint8_t>` to `std::span<uint8_t>` backed by an internal owning storage. Behavior must be unchanged - existing test_simple_memory must pass without edits. Structural-only commit.

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. The TCG plugin build will fail.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")
endif()
